items.headline: Nine new homes proposed for Old Trace Road subdivision
items.summary: The city is reviewing a proposal to subdivide a vacant 1-acre lot into nine single-family homes plus seven junior accessory dwelling units on Old Trace Road. This would add 16 residential units to the Residential Estate zone area.

items.headline: Supermicro invests $2.5M in equipment at Rock Avenue facility
items.summary: Tech company Supermicro is upgrading equipment at its 988 Rock Avenue location with a $2.5 million investment. This signals continued tech sector presence and job activity in San José's industrial corridor.

items.headline: Summerhill redevelopment at El Camino Real moves forward
items.summary: Palo Alto Greens is replacing trees as part of the Summerhill project at 4345 El Camino Real, signaling progress on this significant redevelopment. This is one of the major mixed-use projects residents have been watching in the city.

items.headline: Mountain View approves bonds for new public safety building
items.summary: The City Council approved lease revenue bonds to finance a new public safety building project. This represents a significant infrastructure investment that will affect the city's long-term capital spending and property development.

items.headline: City council reviews biodiversity and urban forest plan
items.summary: Council held a study session to review a draft Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan on April 28. This plan will guide how Mountain View manages green space, tree canopy, and wildlife habitat across the city.

items.headline: Mixed-use development proposed: 67 homes and retail near Speedy's Tacos
items.summary: Sunnyvale is considering a 4.22-acre mixed-use project that would add 67 residential units (55 townhomes and 12 apartments) plus 18,535 square feet of retail space, including a new two-story building next to the existing Speedy's Tacos. The project would be subdivided into 17 residential lots and requires special development permits.

items.headline: Campbell raises business license tax rates
items.summary: The City Council approved an ordinance revising business license tax rates in Campbell's municipal code, effective in the upcoming fiscal year. This annual update could affect local business costs and potentially influence operating expenses for companies in the city.

items.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026: City Council approves large stadium signs
items.summary: Santa Clara authorized temporary supergraphic signs on buildings near Levi's Stadium for the 2026 FIFA World Cup events. The city will allow these large-scale advertisements during the limited tournament period.
